Structurally clean: all 36 chapters present, no gaps, no duplicate verse numbers. Not yet individually verse-verified by a human reader against the source -- treat as amber pending that review, not GREEN. CORRECTED 2026-07-19: an independent secondary check found this file actually mixed two different English translations chapter-by-chapter -- a formal register (matching this note's claim) in a minority of chapters, and a Jamaican-Patois-register translation (matching Wikisource's Patois translation of the same book, not apocryphalibrary) in the majority. Josh chose to standardize on the formal register. Re-fetched directly from apocryphalibrary.weebly.com's own dual-translation page layout (each verse shown in both Patois and formal English) for chapters 3,4,5,6,8,9,10,11,13 -- confirmed formal text available and verse counts match exactly, now replaced. For the remaining Patois chapters (14-18,20,21,23-30,32-36), apocryphalibrary's own pages host ONLY the Patois translation -- no formal-register alternative exists on this source for those chapters. Chapter 14 additionally has an unresolved verse-boundary question: the site's page for '1 Meqabyan 14' contains 23 verse-numbered lines of content, not the 4 currently in this file -- unclear whether this file's chapter 14 is truncated or whether the site's chapter divisions don't align 1:1 with this file's. Both open questions held for Josh's decision, not resolved by guessing.

verses.text: A certain man named ᵇ Tseerutsaydan loved his sin. He would often boast of the great profusion of his troops and of the great armies which he held governance over.
He had countless priests who served the false gods which he worshiped, and [he would often bow to them and make sacrifices by night and day.
In the sightlessness of his own heart, he believed that they granted him power and strength.
He believed that they gave him sovereignty over the nations.
And as his sway over the nations progressed, he believed that [these false gods] granted him the right to supremacy.
[The King] would offer sacrifices to them by night and day,
appointing priests to serve these idols.
These priests ᵃ ate from the unclean sacrifices, all the while telling the king that the idols had consumed what was offered.
They sought to ᵃ proselytize among the people, so that they might make greater sacrifices to be consumed.
The king trusted in these idols, which can grant no favors, nor yield any merit to his welfare.

At the time, when his heart was blind to the truth, [the king] believed that they had given him to fate, by placing him with a diadem. It would appear to him that [these idols] had created him, for ᵃ Satan blocked him from knowledge of the true God, who alone grants eternal life. Rather, [Satan] sought to bring him away from life, into the fire of ᵇ Gehenna forever, for he believed the idols to be gods,
but since they do not truly live, they cannot give life.

It was Satan’s tyranny that mislead them, which was found within their idolatry. He gave of them false speech, and said he would reveal great truths to them, and for this he was loved. [Therefore, Satan] cast the people’s discernment upon the false gods and those that believe in them, and among all the children of Adam, who’s prayers had become like that of the dust.

They gazed upon [the idols] in solemn reverence, and [Satan] filled their mind with their own desires. And the people complied, offering as sacrifice their young girls and young boys, children of their own blood. They spilled the innocent blood of their children.

This did not concern [the king], for Satan had savored the sacrifices he made to his gods in order to complete his malicious designs. [Satan ᵃ sought to] bring him down into Gehenna, where he dwells, and where there is no respite for all eternity, and where all will receive chastisement.
This king, Tseerutsaydan, was full of conceit; for he had fifty idols made of false gods, and twenty idols of false goddesses.
He would often boast about his idolatry, and offered glorification and praise to the idols while he made his daily sacrifices.

He commanded the people, that they too might make sacrifices to these idols. [The king] would eat from these unclean sacrifices, and ᵃ ordered that the people do the same, to spread the evil which he created.
Now, [the king] had five workshops which were under his command, where he forged the idols out of iron, brass and lead.
The king had then adorned in silver and in gold, and had veiled curtains around his palaces for them, each hidden in a tabernacle.

He appointed guardians for their care, and would make offerings of meat to these idols from forty creatures—ten fattened oxen and ten fatted ewes, ten barren calves and ten barren goats, along with winged birds of the air.
It appeared to [the king] that these idols ate and drank, consuming fifty bunches of grapes and fifty dishes of bread kneaded with oil.

The king said to the priests, “Take this, give it to them. Let my gods consume the meat that has been slaughtered on their account. Make them drink from of the wine I have given to them. If this is not enough to quench their desire, I will give them more.”
[The king] then ordered that everyone should eat and drink from that defiling sacrifice.

He then, in his corruption, sent out his armies to march on all the nations of his realm, to find one who would neither make sacrifices, nor ᵃ prostrate themselves [before the idols], so that they could be brought before him. He sought to punish them by ᵇ fire and by sword, [for he feared that the gods] might seize his wealth and burn his palaces. [The king] lived in ᶜ terror that they might remove his great fortune and destroy him.
“My gods are generous and great, and out of their benevolence I have forged them. Therefore, I will give chastisement on him that does not worship my gods and make sacrifices to them.
I will show punish punishment and retribution! For they have created the heavens and the earth, the great wide sea, the moon and sun, the rains and the wind, and all that abide in this world.
Those who will not worship shall be punished under the penalty of law, and I shall have no mercy.
ᵃ There were one man birthed from the tribe of Binyam whose name are called Meqabees;
him had three childran who were handsome an totally warriors ~ them had bein iloved alongside all persons ina that Midyam an Miedon country that are Tseerutsaydan Rule.
An like unto the king commanded them pon the time him found them:—"Don't unu bow fe Tseerutsaydan irators? How about don't unu sacrifice sacrifice?
But if unu refuse—wi will seize an tek unu toward the king—an wi will downstroy all your money like unto the king commanded."

These youts who were handsome replied fe him sayin—"As fe Him fe Whom InI bow—there are InI Faada Irator Who Irated Earth an Heaven an what are within she—an the sea—moon an Sun an clouds an stars ~ Him are the True Irator Whom InI worship an ina Whom InI believe."
An these the king youts are four—an them servants who carry shield an spear are a hundred.

An pon the time them loved that them might seize these hola ones—them escaped from them hands and there are none who touched them ~ as those youts are totally warriors ina Power—them went seizin shields an them spears.
An there were from them one who strangle an kill panther—an at that time him would strangle it like unto a chicken.
An there were one from them who kill a lion with one rock or strikin at one time with a stick.
An there were one from them who kill a hundred persons—strikin ina formation time with one sword—an them name an them hunt were thus ~ it were called ina all Babilon an Mo`ab countries.
An them were warriors ina Power—an them had a thing bein iloved an comeliness.
An again them features comeliness were wondrous—however becau them worshipped JAH an becau them didn't fear death—it are them reasonin comeliness that surpass all.
An pon the time them frightened the troops—there are none who could able fe seize them—but them who were warriors escaped proceedin toward a lofty mountain.

An those troops returned toward the city an shut the fortress gate ~ them terrorized the people sayin—"Unless unu brought those warriors the Meqabyans—wi will burn your city ina fiyah—an wi will send toward the king an downstroy your country."

An at that time the country persons—rich an poor ones an dawtaz an males—a child whose faada an mother dead pon him an old dawtaz—everyone proceeded an shouted together—an them straightened them necks toward the mountain an shouted toward them sayin—"Don't downstroy InI—an don't downstroy InI country pon InI."
At that time them wept together—an them feared—arisin from JAH.
Returnin them faces Eastward an streachin forth them hands them begged toward JAH together—"Lord—should InI refuse these men who demolished Thy Command an Thy LAW?
Yet him believed ina silver an gold an ina the stone an wood that a person hands worked—but InI don't love that InI might hear that criminal word—who didn't believe Thy LAW" them said.
"When Thou are the Irator Who save an Who kill—him mek him ras self like unto them Irated him also ~ as fe him—him are who spill a person blood an who eat a person flesh.
But InI don't love that InI might sight up that criminal face nor hear him word" them said.

"However if Thou commanded InI—InI will go toward him ~ becau InI believe ina Thee-I—InI will pass an give InI bodies fe death—an pon the time him said 'Sacrifice sacrifice fe mi irators'—InI won't hear that criminal word.
But InI believed Thee-I—Lord Who examine kidneys an reasonins—InI Faadas Irator—'Abriham an Yis'haq an Ya`iqob who did Thy Accord an lived firmed up ina Thy LAW.
Thou examine a person reasonin an help the sinner an the righteous one—an there be none hidden from Thee-I—an him who took refuge are revealed alongside Thee-I.
But InI have no other Irator apart from Thee-I.
That InI might give InI bodies fe death becau Thy glorified Name—however be Power an Firmness an a Shelter fe InI ina this Work that InI are ruled fe Thee-I.
An pon the time 'Isra'iel entered toward Gibts country Thou heard Ya`iqob plea—an now glorified God—InI beg Thee-I."

An pon the time the two men whose features were quite handsome were sight up fe them standin before them—pon the time fiyah swords that frighten like unto lightnin alit an cut them necks an killed them—at that time them arose bein well like unto formerly.
Them features comeliness became totally handsome an them shone more than Sun—an them became more handsome than formerly.

As thou seest before thee, the servants of the Most High Jehovah, Abijah, Seelah, Fentos who were dead and arose, you have that you might likewise arise from the dead, and your faces shall shine like unto the Sun in the Kingdom of Heaven.
And they went with those men and received martyrdom there.
And at that time they begged, they praised, and they bowed before Jehovah—death did not frighten them and the king's punishment did not frighten them.

And they went toward those youths and became like unto a sheep that has no evil, yet they did not frighten them, and upon the time that they arrived, they seized and beat them and bound and whipped them, and they delivered them up to the king and placed them before him.
And the king answered them saying, "Why will ye stubborn ones not sacrifice sacrifices and bow before my creators?"
Those brethren who were cleansed from sin, who were honored and chosen and joyful, and who shined like a jewel of wondrous value, Seelah and Abijah and Fentos answered him in one word.
They told that king who was a plague, "As for us, we will not bow nor sacrifice unto defouled idols that have not knowledge nor heart."

And again, they told him, "We will not bow before idols of silver and gold that a man has wrought with his hands, of stone and wood, that have no heart nor soul nor knowledge, that do not benefit their friends nor harm their enemies."
And the king answered them, saying, "Why doest thou thus, as they know who insults them and who wrongs them? Why do you insult the glorified creators?
They answered him, saying, "They are like unto a trifle alongside us; as for us, we will insult them and will not glorify them.
And the king answered them, saying, "I will punish thee with an evil measure according to your works; I will destroy your comeliness with whipping and firm tribulation and fire.
And now tell me whether ye will give or not give sacrifice unto my creators, and if ye do not this, I will punish you by the sword and by whipping."

They answered him, saying, "As for us, we will not sacrifice nor bow unto defouled idols." And the king commanded them that they might beat them with a fat stick, and again, that they might whip them with a whip, and after that, that they might tear their flesh until their innards might be seen.
And after this they bound them and kept them in prison until he counseled with money that he might punish and kill them.
Without mercy they took them and bound them securely in prison, and they sat in prison for three nights and three days.
And after the third day the king commanded that a proclamation speaker might turn, and that counselors and nobles, local elders and officials, might be gathered.
And at that time Tseerutsaydan sat in the square; he commanded that they might bring forth those honored ones, Seelah and Abijah and Fentos; they stood before him, wounded and bound.
And the king said unto them, "When you sat (in prison) these three days, will you recall (your words), or will you remain in your former evil?"
And those honored soldiers of Jehovah answered him saying, "Even with this cruelty, we will not agree to worship the idols filled with sin that thou dost regard.
And that man of lawlessness was vexed and commanded them to stand them up in a lofty place and renew their wounds, and their blood flowed upon the Earth.

And again he commanded them that they might burn them with a torch lamp and might char their flesh, and his servants did as he commanded them. And those honored men said unto him, "Thou who didst not remember the Law of Jehovah, behold, our reward shall abound in the measure whereby thou multipliest our punishment."

And again, he commanded that they might bring and send forth upon them bears and tigers and lions: evil beasts before them to consume them, that they might utterly consume their flesh with their bones.

And he commanded the beastkeepers that they might send the beasts upon them, and they did according to his word, and they bound those honored martyrs' feet, and again, they maliciously beat them and bound them with tent-stakes.
And those beasts were flung over them while they roared, and when they reached the martyrs they hailed and bowed unto them.
They returned unto the keepers while they roared and frightened the keepers. They took them toward the square until they delivered them up before the king.
And there they killed seventy-five men from the army of lawless men.
Many men panicked, the one in fear and anguish upon the other, until the king fled from his throne; and they seized the beasts with difficulty and took them toward their lodging.

And two of the brethren of Seelah and Abijah and Fentos came and released them from prison, they released their bonds and said unto them, "Come, let us flee lest these unbelievers and lawless ones find us."
And these martyrs answered their brethren, saying," It is not fit that we flee after we are set up for a testimony; if you are afraid, then go and flee.
And those their younger brethren said, "We will stand with you before the king, and if you perish, we will perish with you also."

And after this the king was upon his lordship hall balcony and saw that these honored men were released, and that all five brethren stood together. Those chiefs of the troops wondered if they were brethren and told the king. And the king was vexed and shouted like unto a wild boar.

And until the king offered money for the punishment of the five brethren, he commanded that his men might seize them and cast them into prison. And they cast them into prison and bound them firmly, and without mercy they treated them with a hollow stalk.

And the king Tseerutsaydan said, "These youths who have erred have wearied me. Why should these men harden their hearts? They work evil and are like unto power and firmness; If I say, 'they will return', they will make their hearts evil.

And I will bring hardship and an evil measure upon them like unto their works, and I will burn their flesh in the fire that it might be charred ash, and I will scatter their ashes like unto dust upon the mountains.

And after he said this he waited three days and commanded them that they might bring those honored men. And when those honored men approached him he commanded that they might be burned in a fire within a great pit oven, and that they might add to it a malicious work that enflames the fire. And thereby they boiled a vat; the fat and soapberries, seafoam and resin and sulfur.
And when the fire burned in the pit, the messengers went unto the king and said, "We have done that which thou commanded us; we send the men who will be cast therein."

And he commanded them that they might receive them and cast them into the pit of fire, and the youths did as the king commanded them. And when those honored men entered into the fire, they gave their souls to Jehovah.

And when the men who cast them therein looked up, they saw that angels received them and took their souls to the Garden where Isaac and Abraham and Jacob are, where the blessed Israelites are found. Previous chapter Next chapter

And when that lawless one saw that they were dead, he commanded them to burn their flesh with fire until they were ash and to scatter them in the wind. But the fire was not able to burn their corpses from their hair to their side, and they sent them forth from the pit.
And again they burned fire over them from the morning til the evening—and it didn't burn them—and they said: "And now come, let us cast their corpses into the sea."

And they did as the king commanded them—they cast them into the sea—and even if they cast them therein, adding great stones, iron hearthstones, and a millstone whereby a donkey grindeth by turning, it did not sink them, as Jehovah the Spirit of Support was within them; they floated upon the sea, yet they did not sink. He failed to destroy them with all the malice that he exercised upon them.
And he said, "Their death hath wearied me more than their life. I will cast their corpses to the beasts that they may eat them, else what will I do?"

And the youths did as he commanded them, and vultures and beasts did not touch their corpses; birds and vultures protected them from burning in the sun with their wings. And the five martyrs' corpses sat fourteen days.
And when they looked upon them, their bodies shone like the sun, and angels encircled about their corpses like light encircling the Tent.
And he sought counsel, for he knew not what he should do. After this he dug a grave and buried the five martyrs.
And when the king, who had forgotten the Law of Jehovah, reclined upon a bed at night, the five martyrs appeared to him standing before him at night, vexing him and wielding swords.

And it seemed to him that they entered into his house by night in a crime; when he awoke from his sleep he was afraid, and he desired to flee from the bedchamber toward the hall. And it seemed to him that they would kill him, as it seemed they committed a crime against him, and he feared and his knees trembled.
Because of this thing he said, "My lords, what do you desire? As for me, what shall I do for you."

They answered him, saying, "Are we not they whom thou slayed by burning with fire, and they whom thou commanded that they might be cast into the sea? As Jehovah hath preserved out bodies because we believed in him, thou hast failed to destroy us. As one who believed in Him will not perish—glory and praise is due to Jehovah—so did we and also those who believed in Him not be ashamed in tribulation."
(And he said unto them,) "I did not know that a punishment like this would find me, therefore, what reward shall I give unto you for recompense because of the evil thing I have done to you?
And now take from me the reward I shall give unto you; lest ye take my body in death, and lest ye lower my body toward Sheol, while I am yet in life.
As I have wronged you, forgive me my sin, because mercy is the Law of thy Father Jehovah." Thus he said unto them.

And those honored martyrs answered him, saying, "Because of thy deed wherefore thou dist an evil thing unto us, as for us, we will not reward you with an evil thing, as Jehovah is He Who bringeth hardship upon a soul; as for Him Who will reward thee with hardship, it is Jehovah.
However we were shown unto thee, revealing that we are well; for thy timeframe is short and thy heart is deaf; Though it seemed to thee that thou slayed us, thou prepared welfare for us.
But thy idols, thy priests, and thou will descend to Gehenna where there is no escape forever.

Woe unto thy idols to whom thou dost bow; thou hast ceased to bow to Jehovah Who created thee when thou wast scorned like unto spittle, and unto all of thee who worship them; and ye know not that Jehovah Who created you bringeth forth the living from the non-living. Art thou not they who are seen today as smoke, and he who tomorrow wilt perish?"
And the king answered them, saying, "What commandest thou me that I might do unto you all that thou desirest?"
"It is to save thyself lest thou enter toward the Gehenna of Fire, yet it is not to save ourselves who teach thee.
For thy idols are silver and gold, stone and wood; that have no heart, nor soul, nor knowledge; which the hand of a man has worked.

But they do not kill, they do not save; they do not benefit their friend, they do not harm their enemy; they do not debase, they do not honor; they do not make wealthy, they do not impoverish; they mislead you by the authority of demons, who do not desire that men might be saved; yet they do not uproot nor plant.
They especially do not desire that men like unto thee might be saved from death, thou dull-hearted ones to whom it seemth that they created thee, when thou are those who worked them.
As Satans' and demons' authority hath lodged in them, they shall return to thee that which thou desired, that they might drown you in the sea of Gehenna.
But thou, cease thou this error and let this also be our reward for our death, that we might benefit our souls worshiping our Creator, Jehovah." Thus they said unto him.
But he was alarmed and utterly astonished, and because he beheld all five of them draw their swords, he was afraid, and because of this thing he bowed unto them.
(And he said,) "Hence I know that after the dead who were dust are dead, they will truly arise; as for me, only a little (time) remaineth before I am dead."
After this they were hidden before the king's face; from that day onward that Tseerutsaydan was completely arrogant ceased burning their corpses.

As they have misled him in various times, he would be happy in his idols, and his heart (would) err. And he misled many people like unto him until he ceased following and worshiped Jehovah Who created him, yet it is not only him who erred.

And they would sacrifice their daughters and their sons unto demons, yet they work a seduction and a disturbance according to their reasoning, that which their father Satan taught them that he might work seduction and disturbance that Jehovah doth not love.

They marry their mothers, and abuse their aunts and their sisters, they abuse their bodies while they worked all that resembleth this filthy work; as Satan hath reinforced those crooked mens' hearts, they said, "We will not return."
But that Tseerutsaydan, who knoweth not his Creator, was completely arrogant, and he would boast in his idols.
If they say, "How will Jehovah give the Kingdom to those men who know Him not in Law nor in Worship?" they will fully return to Him in repentance. As He trieth them thus, it is because of this.
But if they fully return in repentance He will love them, and He will keep their Kingdom, but if they refuse a fire will punish them in the Fire of Gehenna forever.

But it would be due to a king who feareth his Creator Jehovah like unto his lordship's fame, and it would be due to a judge to be ruled by his Creator while he judged righteous judgement like unto the fame of his rule's fame.
And it would be due to elders and chiefs and envoys and petty kings to be commanded by their Creator like unto their lordships' abundant measure.

As He is Lord of Heaven and Earth, Who created all creation, because there is no other Creator in Heaven nor Earth who impoverish and maketh rich; it is He who honoreth and debaseth. Previous chapter Next chapter
The one warrior from the sixty warriors was proud; Jehovah made his body from his foot to his head to swell with one spoon of sulphur; he died in one plague.
And again Keeram who built an iron bed was proud because of his power and abundance, and Jehovah hid him in death.

And again Nebuchadnezzar was prideful, saying, "There is no other king other than me, and I am the creator who maketh the sun arise in this world," and the said thus because of his arrogance and abundance

And Jehovah separated him from men and sent him toward a wilderness for seven years, and he made his fortune with birds of Heaven and beasts of the wilderness up until he knew that it was Jehovah Who created him.
And at the time he knew Him in worship, He again returned him to his kingdom. Who is it who is not of the Earth, being boldly prideful against Jehovah Who created him?
Or who is it who demolished His Law and His Order whom the Earth did not swallow?

And thou, Tseerutsaydan, desirest that thou might be prideful against thy Creator, and again, thou hast that He might destroy thee like unto them, and might lower thee into thy grave because of thy arrogance.

And again, after they entered into Sheol where there is grinding of teeth and mourning, that were there is a fullness of darkness, thou hast that He might lower thee into the deep pit of Gehenna where there is no escape forever.
As for thee, thou art a man who will die and be demolished tomorrow like unto the arrogant kings who were like unto thee, who left this world of the living.
As for us, we say: "Thou art demolished ruins, but thou art not not Jehovah, for it is Jehovah who created the Earth and Heaven and thee.
He debaseth arrogant ones, He honoreth those who were debased; He giveth forgiveness to men who art wearied.
He slayeth them that are well, He raiseth up those who were dust, who died and were buried in the grave.
And He sendeth forth slaves free in Life from the rule of sin.
O king Tseerutsaydan, why do ye boast in thy defouled idols who have no benefit?
But Jehovah created Earth and Heaven and great seas, He created the moon and the Sun, and He prepared the times.
Man grazeth in his field, and all the while he ploweth up until dusk, and the stars of Heaven live securely by His Word.
And he calleth all in Heaven; there is nothing done without Jehovah knowing it.
He commanded the Angels of Heaven that they might serve Him and might praise His glorified Name, and Angels are sent to all men who inherit Life.
ᵃ Rafael who was a servant was sent to Tobit, and he ᵇ saved Tobias from death in Raguel's country.
Holy Michael was sent to Gideon that he might draw his attention with money that he destroy the Philistines, and he was sent to the prophet Moses at the time he made Israel cross the Eritrean sea.
As only Jehovah has said, He led them; there was no other idol with them
And He sent them forth toward crops apon the earth.
And He fed them the grain of His plantation, because He loved them completely, He cherished them, feeding them honey that hardened like a rock.
And that thou might completely keep His kindred by what they are due, and that thou might do the Will of Jehovah Who created thee, He crowned thee, giving (thee) authority over the four kingdoms
For He hath crowned thee, making (thee) loftier than all, and thy Creator verily crowned the that thou might love Jehovah.

And it is right that thou might love thy Creator Jehovah how He loved thee, how He trusted thee with all the people, and thou, do the Will of Jehovah that thy time may abound in this world and that He might live with thee in support.
And do Jehovah's Will that He might stand for thee, being a Guardian upon thine enemies, and that He might seat the upon they throne, and that He might hide thee under His Wing of Support.

As for thee, if thou knowest not, Jehovah crowned thee upon Israel like He chose Saul from the children of Israel when he kept his father's donkeys, and He crowned him upon his kindred, Israel, and he sat with Israel upon his throne.
And He gave him a lofty fortune, separating him from his kindred; Jehovah crowned thee upon His kindred; as for henceforth and onward, watch, keep His kindred.

As Jehovah hath appointed thee over them that thou might ᵃ kill and might save, keep them in evil things, they who work a goodly thing and they who work an evil thing upon a goodly thing." Thus he said unto him.

"And as Jehovah hath appointed thee over all that thou might do His Will, be it while thou art whipped or while thou art saved, repay their evil works, they who work goodly works, and they who (both) work goodly works and work evil works.

For thou art a servant of Jehovah Who ruleth all in Heaven, and thou, do Jehovah's Will that He might do thy will for thee in all thy thoughts and in all that thou beggedst whil thou wheedled before Him.
There is none who ruleth Him, but He ruleth all.
There is none who appointeth Him, but He appointeth all.
There is none who dismisseth Him, but He dismisseth all.
There is non who reproacheth Him, but He reproacheth all.

There is non who maketh Him diligent, but He maketh all diligent, for the rulership of Heaven and Earth is for Him. There is none who escapeth from His authority; all is revealed alongside Him, yet there is nothing hidden from His Face.
He beholdeth all, but there is none who beholdeth Him, He heareth the prayer of the man who prayeth to Him saying, 'Save me', for He hath created man in His Image, and He accepteth his plea.

For He is a King Who liveth until Eternity; He feedeth all from His unchanging Nature. 19a Tob. 3:17 b Tob. 8:1–3 30a 1 Esd. 4:7 Previous chapter Next chapter Powered by Create your own unique website with customizable templates. As He verily crowneth the kings who do His Will, the kings wrote an honest word because of Him.

As they have done the Will of Jehovah, He shall with an immeasurable light cause to shine Isaac and Abraham and Jacob, Solomon and David and Hezekiah lodging in the Garden where all beautiful kings whose lodgings were in the light.
It is Heaven's Hall that verily shone, yet Earth's halls are not like Heaven's Hall. Its floor, whose features are made of silver and gold and jewels, are clean.
And its features that verily shine are unsearchable by human hearts; Heaven's Hall is that which shineth like unto jewels.

As Jehovah knew, who is a Knower of Natures, the Hall of Heaven that He created is that which a man's heart doth not comprehend and that which shineth in complete light. Its floor, that was made from silver and gold, from jewels, from white silk and blue silk, is clean.
It is verily and completely beautiful like unto this.
Righteous men who are made firm in religion and virtue are they who shall inherit it through the Charity and Pardon of Jehovah.
And there is healing Water that floweth from it, and it verily shineth like unto the Sun, and there is a great tabernacle of Light within it, and it is encircled about by the perfume of grace.

And a Garden of fruit that is beautiful and beloved, whose features and taste are unique, surround the house, and there are oil and grapes placed their, and it is very beautiful, and the fragrance of its fruit is sweet.
When a man of flesh and blood enters it, his soul would that he separate from his flesh, and from the abundance of the blessed Israelites that in it, due to its fragrant flavor.
Beautiful kings who did the Will of Jehovah shall be blessed there; their honor and their places are known in the Kingdom of Heaven that live steadfastly forever, where welfare is found.

He showed that their lordship upon the Earth was famed an honored, and that their lordship in Heaven was famed an honored; They shall be honored and lofty in Heaven as they honored them and bowed to them in this world. If they work good Works in this world then they shall be blessed.

But kings who were evil in their rule and their kingdoms that Jehovah gave them, they do not judge honestly according to what is due; as they have heeded not the cries of the destitute and the poor ones, they do not judge Truth and save the refugee and the wronged child whose father and mother have died.

They save not the destitute and poor ones from the wealthy hand that robbeth them; they do not divide and give food and rest to them who are hungred, and they divide and give not from their drink and give not drink to those who thirsted, and they did not turn their ears to the poor one's cry.

And he shall bring them to Gehenna for a dark ending, when that lofty Day arriveth upon them and when Jehovah shall come; and at that time His wrath will be exercised upon them as David said in his Praises: "Lord, do not chastise me in Thy Judgement, and do not admonish me in Thy chastisement"; Their problems and their debasing shall abound like unto fame and abundant measure.
When nobles and kings ruleth this world, there are those who keep not Thy law.

ABut Jehovah Who ruleth all that there is in Heaven, all mens' souls and all mens' welfare hath been seized by His Authority; It is He Who giveth honor to those who glorify Him—for He verily ruleth all—and He loveth those who love Him.

As He is Lord of Earth and Heaven, He examineth and knoweth what the kidneys transport and what the heart thinketh, and to he who beggeth to Him with pure hearts, He shall give him the reward for his pleas.
AHe shall destroy the arrogance of the powerful ones, who work evil works upon the child whose mother and father are dead, and upon old women.

It is not by thy power that thou hast seized this kingdom—it is not by thine own ability that thou sat upon this throne—He desired to try thee thus that it might be possible for thee to rule like Saul, who ruled his kindreds in that season; and He seated thee upon the throne of a kingdom, yet it is not by thine own power, it is when He tryeth thee like unto Saul, who heeded not the words of the prophet Samuel and the Word of Jehovah, and did not serve his army nor the king of Amalek; yet it is not by thine own ability that thou seized this kingdom.

And Jehovah told the prophet Samuel, "Go, and because they have grievedst me by demolishing the Law, and worshipping idols, and bowing to the idol, and by their synagogues, and by all their hated Works with no benefit; tell Saul, "Go to the nation of Amalek and destroy their hosts and all their kings from the men to the livestock."
Upon those who have grieved Jehovah, because of this thing He sent Saul that he might destroy them.

But he saved their king from death, and he saved many livestock and beauties and women and handsome youths from death; and he scorned My work and did not hear My Commandment; because of this thing, Jehovah told the prophet Samuel, "Go and divide his kingdom."
"In his stead, anoint David the son of Jesse that he might reign over Israel.
But upon him send a demon who will strangle him and cast him out.

For he hath that if I gave him a kingdom that he do My Will, when he refused Me to do My Will I dismissed him from the kingdom that was due to him. But thou, go and tell him saying, 'Wilt thou thus heed not Jehovah Who crowned thee over His kindred Israel, Who hath seated the upon His Lordship's Throne?'
But thou, say unto him, 'Thou didst not know Jehovah Who gave thee this honor and fame,'" He told him.
And the prophet Samuel went unto King Saul, and went unto him sitting at a dinnertable; and Agag, king of Amalek, had sat upon his left.
"Why didst thou heed not at all Jehovah Who commanded the that thou might destroy the livestock and the people?" Thus he said unto him.
And at that time the king feared and arose from his throne saying unto Samuel, "Repent for us" and he seized his clothes. And Samuel refused to repent, and Samuel's clothes were rent.
And Samuel said unto Saul, "Jehovah hath divided thy kingdom.

And again Saul said unto Samuel before the people, "Honor me and atone for my sin before Jehovah, that He may forgive me," and because he feared the Word of Jehovah Who created him, but did not fear the king who was dead, Samuel refused to repent for his words.
Because of this thing he pierced Agag the king of Amalek before he had swallowed what he had chewed.

And a demon seized that Saul who demolished the Law of Jehovah, and because He is the King of Kings Who ruleth all, Jehovah struck upon the head a king who workedst iniquity, for it doth not shame him (to do so).
For He is Lord of all nations Who dismisseth the authority of all the nobles and Kings who fear him not, but there is none who ruleth Him.
Likewise He spoke, saying, David's kindred shall be, as it were, famed and honored, but Saul's kindred shall be, as it were, debased; He destroyed the kingdom Saul and his children.

ABecause it grieved Him, and because He destroyed the lawless ones who grievedst Him with their evil works, Jehovah avenged and destroyed Saul's kindred and his children, for he who doth not avenge the enemy of Jehovah, he is His enemy.

When it is possible for him to avenge and destroy, and when he hath authority, a man who doth not avenge nor destroy the sinner, and doth not avenge and destroy the man who keepeth not the Law of Jehovah, as he is the enemy of Jehovah, so He destroyed Saul's kindred and his children. Hear me, for I will tell the the thing whereby dead men shall arise: they shall plant a plant and be fertile, and grapes shall send forth vines—as Jehovah shall bring forth the fruit (abundant in it?), and shall cast wine from it.
Understand that that plant that thou hast planted was small, but she sent forth buds, fruit, and leaves today.
Jehovah giveth her root to drink from, from both earth and water.
But He feedeth her wood from fire and wind, her roots give her leaves water to drink, and earth giveth her firmness for wood.
But the soul that Jehovah hath created shall bear fruit amidst them, and dead men who shall rise shall do likewise.
At the time that the souls are separated from the flesh, as each of their own selves have gone, He sayeth, "Gather the souls from the four natures, from earth and water, from wind and fire."
But earth's nature lived steadfastly in her nature and became earth, and water lived steadfastly in her nature and became water.
And wind's nature lived steadfastly in her nature and became wind, and fire lived steadfastly in her nature and became a hot fire.

But a soul that Jehovah hath separated from the flesh returneth to her Creator, up until He raiseth her up united with the flesh when He desireth—He placeth her in the Garden in the place which He desireth.

He placeth righteous souls in the house of Light in the Garden, but that he might send away the souls of sinners, He also placeth them in the house of darkness in Sheol until the time that He desireth.
Jehovah told the prophet Ezekiel, "Call the souls from the four corners, that they might be gathered and be one limb.
At the time He spoke in one word saying thus, "the souls were gathered from the four corners."
And the nature of water brought vegetation, and again, the nature of fire brought fire.
And again, the nature of Earth brought Earth, and the nature of wind brought wind.
And Jehovah brought a soul from the Garden where He placed it—they were gathered by one Word, and a Resurrection was made.

And again, I shall show thee the examples that are alongside thee: (at) the day dusk, thou sleepest, and (at) the night dawn, thou risest from thy bed—but at the time thou sleepest it is thy example of death.
And at the time thou wakedst it arose as thine example, but the night when all men sleep whose bodies were dark—for darkness hath covered them—is an example unto the world.
But the morning light—when darkness is eliminated and when light is in all the world, and when men arise and graze toward the field—is an example unto the dead.
And this Kingdom of Heaven when men are renewed like unto this—the Resurrection of the dead is like unto this—as the this world passeth, it is the example of the night.
And as David spoke, saying: " ᵃ He placed His example in the sun", as the ᵇ Sun shineth upon the time that it rose, it is an example of the Kingdom of Heaven.

And as the Sun shineth in the world today, when Christ shall come He shall shine like the Sun in the Kingdom of Heaven that is new; as He hath said: " ᵃ I am a Sun which setteth not, and a Torch that is not extinguished." He, Jehovah, is her Light.

And He shall quickly raise the dead again; I shall bring one example before the again from thy food which thou sowest and whereby thou art saved, and whether it be a wheat kernel or a barley kernel or a lentil kernel or all the seeds that man soweth upon the Earth, there are none that grow ᵃ save it will be demolished and rot.
And like the flesh of man which thou beholdest, when it is demolished and rotten, the Earth doth consume its stoutness with the hide.
And when the Earth doth consume its stoutness and growth around a seventh of a kernel, Jehovah sendeth a cloud that seizeth rain as He desires, and roots grow upon the earth and send forth leaves.
And if she were demolished and rotten she could not grow, but after she grew she sent forth many buds.
And by the Will of Jehovah fruit is sent forth from those buds that grew, and He doth clothe its stoutness in straw.
Behold the measure that the kernel seeds thou hast sowed abounded, yet the silver and the leaf, the ear and the straw, they are not counted unto the.

Do not be a fool who knowest not, and behold thy seed that it hath abounded—and all likewise—know thou that the dead shall receive the Resurrection that they shall arise, and their punishments according to their Works.

Hear me, if thou sowest wheat, it will not grow and be barley, nor will it be wheat if thou sowest barley—and let me tell the again that it will not grow. If thou sowest wheat, wilt thou gather barley? If thou sowest watercress, wilt thou gather linseed?
And what about kinds of plants? If thou plantest figs, will they grow for the into nuts? Or if thou plantest almonds, will they grow unto thee as grapes?
If thou plant sweet fruit will it grow and be bitter? How, if thou plantest bitter fruit, is it possible for it to be sweet?

Likewise, how, if a sinner is dead, is it possible (for him) to arise and be righteous at the time of the ᵃ Resurrection ? How, if a righteous man is dead, is it possible (for him) to arise as a sinner at the time of the Resurrection? Every man shall receive his punishment according to his works, yet he will receive his punishment according to his sin and the works of his hands, yet there are none who will be convicted by the ᵇ sin of his companion.
A highland tree is planted and it sendeth forth long branches and it will completely dry up; unless Heaven raineth rain its leaves will not be green.
And the cedar will be uprooted from its roots unless the summer rain lighteth upon it.

And like unto all this, the dead will not arise unless the dew of welfare lighteth upon them as commanded by Jehovah. 20a Ps. 19:4 . And likewise, pardon and dew that are commanded by Jehovah do not light upon doubters and lawless ones who work error and lawlessness beforehand—dead men shall not arise—and Deemas and Cyprus who worship idols and dig up roots and instigate a thing,
And they who dig up roots and practice sorcery and cause men to battle,

These are they who shall be convicted on the day when the dead (shall) arise and when Final Judgment is done; yet men who save their own selves and who lust after the work of their own hands, they err by their idols.
Thou vain of heart, (thou) foolish one, doth it appear unto thee that the dead will not rise?

At the time a trumpet is blown by the tongue of the Chief of the angels, Holy Michael, that the dead shall arise then; as thou wilt not remain in the grave without arising, do not believe anything that is thus.

However, were it not so, it would be that men of aforetimes would be buried in the grave of their fathers—Beginning from Adam, beginning from Seth and Abel, Shem and Noah, Isaac and Abraham, Joseph and Jacob, and Aaron and Moses—yet for what (reason) did they not desire that they might be buried in another place?

Is it not so they might arise together with their cousins at the time of the Resurrection? Or is it not lest their bones be counted with the bones of evil ones and pagans, they who worship idols? For what (reason) did they not desire that they might be buried in another place?

But thou, do not lead thine own heart astray whilst thou say, "How will the dead arise after they are dead, they who were buried in one grave being tens of thousands and whose bodies are destroyed and rotten?
And when thou lookedst toward a grave, thou didst say this in the dullness of thy heart whilst thou said: "A whole fistful of Earth will not be found, how shall the dead arise?"
Wilt thou say that the seed which thou sowest will not grow? Even the seed which thou sowest shall grow.

an all that live within Water - all that fly pon wind - yet I-man aren't Who feed only man ~ all this are I money.

an fe all peoples idols ina them area - them would sacrifice sacrifice; an all 'Isra'iel would worship idols like unto peoples worship idols by money that them sight up an heard ~ them would mek them games an them songs an them bluster that peoples mek.
